#### BetaMessagesBatches

##### [Create a Message Batch](api/beta/messages/batches/create.md)

beta.messages.batches.create(BatchCreateParams\*\*kwargs)  -> [BetaMessageBatch](api/beta.md)

POST/v1/messages/batches

##### [Retrieve a Message Batch](api/beta/messages/batches/retrieve.md)

beta.messages.batches.retrieve(strmessage\_batch\_id, BatchRetrieveParams\*\*kwargs)  -> [BetaMessageBatch](api/beta.md)

GET/v1/messages/batches/{message\_batch\_id}

##### [List Message Batches](api/beta/messages/batches/list.md)

beta.messages.batches.list(BatchListParams\*\*kwargs)  -> SyncPage[[BetaMessageBatch](api/beta.md)]

GET/v1/messages/batches

##### [Cancel a Message Batch](api/beta/messages/batches/cancel.md)

beta.messages.batches.cancel(strmessage\_batch\_id, BatchCancelParams\*\*kwargs)  -> [BetaMessageBatch](api/beta.md)

POST/v1/messages/batches/{message\_batch\_id}/cancel

##### [Delete a Message Batch](api/beta/messages/batches/delete.md)

beta.messages.batches.delete(strmessage\_batch\_id, BatchDeleteParams\*\*kwargs)  -> [BetaDeletedMessageBatch](api/beta.md)

DELETE/v1/messages/batches/{message\_batch\_id}

##### [Retrieve Message Batch results](api/beta/messages/batches/results.md)

beta.messages.batches.results(strmessage\_batch\_id, BatchResultsParams\*\*kwargs)  -> [BetaMessageBatchIndividualResponse](api/beta.md)

GET/v1/messages/batches/{message\_batch\_id}/results

##### ModelsExpand Collapse

оЂЄ

class BetaMessageBatch: вЂ¦о‚љ

оЂЄ

id: strо‚љ

Unique object identifier.

The format and length of IDs may change over time.

archived\_at: Optional[datetime]о‚љ

RFC 3339 datetime string representing the time at which the Message Batch was archived and its results became unavailable.

cancel\_initiated\_at: Optional[datetime]о‚љ

RFC 3339 datetime string representing the time at which cancellation was initiated for the Message Batch. Specified only if cancellation was initiated.

created\_at: datetimeо‚љ

RFC 3339 datetime string representing the time at which the Message Batch was created.

оЂЄ

ended\_at: Optional[datetime]о‚љ

RFC 3339 datetime string representing the time at which processing for the Message Batch ended. Specified only once processing ends.

Processing ends when every request in a Message Batch has either succeeded, errored, canceled, or expired.

formatdate-time

expires\_at: datetimeо‚љ

RFC 3339 datetime string representing the time at which the Message Batch will expire and end processing, which is 24 hours after creation.

оЂЄ

processing\_status: Literal["in\_progress", "canceling", "ended"]о‚љ

Processing status of the Message Batch.

One of the following:

"in\_progress"о‚љ

"canceling"о‚љ

"ended"о‚љ

оЂЄ

request\_counts: [BetaMessageBatchRequestCounts](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Tallies requests within the Message Batch, categorized by their status.

Requests start as `processing` and move to one of the other statuses only once processing of the entire batch ends. The sum of all values always matches the total number of requests in the batch.

оЂЄ

canceled: intо‚љ

Number of requests in the Message Batch that have been canceled.

This is zero until processing of the entire Message Batch has ended.

оЂЄ

errored: intо‚љ

Number of requests in the Message Batch that encountered an error.

This is zero until processing of the entire Message Batch has ended.

оЂЄ

expired: intо‚љ

Number of requests in the Message Batch that have expired.

This is zero until processing of the entire Message Batch has ended.

processing: intо‚љ

Number of requests in the Message Batch that are processing.

оЂЄ

succeeded: intо‚љ

Number of requests in the Message Batch that have completed successfully.

This is zero until processing of the entire Message Batch has ended.

оЂЄ

results\_url: Optional[str]о‚љ

URL to a `.jsonl` file containing the results of the Message Batch requests. Specified only once processing ends.

Results in the file are not guaranteed to be in the same order as requests. Use the `custom_id` field to match results to requests.

оЂЄ

type: Literal["message\_batch"]о‚љ

Object type.

For Message Batches, this is always `"message_batch"`.

оЂЄ

Skill version or 'latest' for most recent version

оЂЄ

content: List[[BetaContentBlock](api/beta.md)]о‚љ

Content generated by the model.

This is an array of content blocks, each of which has a `type` that determines its shape.

Example:

```shiki
[{"type": "text", "text": "Hi, I'm Claude."}]
```

оЃ–

If the request input `messages` ended with an `assistant` turn, then the response `content` will continue directly from that last turn. You can use this to constrain the model's output.

For example, if the input `messages` were:

```shiki
[
  {"role": "user", "content": "What's the Greek name for Sun? (A) Sol (B) Helios (C) Sun"},
  {"role": "assistant", "content": "The best answer is ("}
]
```

оЃ–

Then the response `content` might be:

```shiki
[{"type": "text", "text": "B)"}]
```

оЃ–

One of the following:

оЂЄ

citations: Optional[List[[BetaTextCitation](api/beta.md)]]о‚љ

Citations supporting the text block.

The type of citation returned will depend on the type of document being cited. Citing a PDF results in `page_location`, plain text results in `char_location`, and content document results in `content_block_location`.

One of the following:

class BetaCitationContentBlockLocation: вЂ¦о‚љ

оЂЄ

cited\_text: strо‚љ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

document\_index: intо‚љ

document\_title: Optional[str]о‚љ

оЂЄ

end\_block\_index: intо‚љ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

file\_id: Optional[str]о‚љ

start\_block\_index: intо‚љ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

type: Literal["content\_block\_location"]о‚љ

оЂЄ

citations: Optional[List[[BetaTextCitation](api/beta.md)]]о‚љ

Citations supporting the text block.

The type of citation returned will depend on the type of document being cited. Citing a PDF results in `page_location`, plain text results in `char_location`, and content document results in `content_block_location`.

One of the following:

class BetaCitationContentBlockLocation: вЂ¦о‚љ

оЂЄ

cited\_text: strо‚љ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

document\_index: intо‚љ

document\_title: Optional[str]о‚љ

оЂЄ

end\_block\_index: intо‚љ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

file\_id: Optional[str]о‚љ

start\_block\_index: intо‚љ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

type: Literal["content\_block\_location"]о‚љ

оЂЄ

оЂЄ

end\_block\_index: intо‚љ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

оЂЄ

search\_result\_index: intо‚љ

0-based index of the cited search result among all `search_result` content blocks in the request, in the order they appear across messages and tool results.

Counted separately from `document_index`; server-side web search results are not included in this count.

class BetaCompactionBlock: вЂ¦о‚љ

A compaction block returned when autocompact is triggered.

When content is None, it indicates the compaction failed to produce a valid
summary (e.g., malformed output from the model). Clients may round-trip
compaction blocks with null content; the server treats them as no-ops.

content: Optional[str]о‚љ

Summary of compacted content, or null if compaction failed

encrypted\_content: Optional[str]о‚љ

Opaque metadata from prior compaction, to be round-tripped verbatim

type: Literal["compaction"]о‚љ

оЂЄ

class BetaFallbackBlock: вЂ¦о‚љ

Marks the point in `content` where one model's output gives way to the next.

One block appears per hop where a preceding model actually ran this turn and
declined. A turn routed directly by the sticky decision has no such boundary
and carries no block вЂ” the signal for whether a fallback model served the
response is the presence of a `fallback_message` entry in
`usage.iterations`, not this block.

The block is treated like a server-tool content block for streaming: it
arrives via the standard `content_block_start` / `content_block_stop`
pair and carries no deltas.

оЂЄ

from\_: [BetaFallbackInfo](api/beta.md)о‚љ

The model whose output ends at this point вЂ” the model that declined at this hop. When the declining hop is the requested model, its `model` echoes the top-level `model` string the caller sent (alias or canonical); when the declining hop is a fallback model, its `model` is that model's canonical id.

оЂЄ

model: [Model](api/messages.md)о‚љ

The model that will complete your prompt.

See [models](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/models-overview) for additional details and options.

strо‚љ

оЂЄ

role: Literal["assistant"]о‚љ

Conversational role of the generated message.

This will always be `"assistant"`.

оЂЄ

stop\_details: Optional[BetaRefusalStopDetails]о‚љ

Structured information about a refusal.

оЂЄ

category: Optional[Literal["cyber", "bio", "reasoning\_extraction"]]о‚љ

The policy category that triggered the refusal.

`null` when the refusal doesn't map to a named category.

One of the following:

"cyber"о‚љ

"bio"о‚љ

"reasoning\_extraction"о‚љ

оЂЄ

explanation: Optional[str]о‚љ

Human-readable explanation of the refusal.

This text is not guaranteed to be stable. `null` when no explanation is available for the category.

оЂЄ

fallback\_credit\_token: Optional[str]о‚љ

Opaque code that refunds the cache-miss cost when retrying this refused
request on the fallback model. Pass it as `fallback_credit_token` on the
retry request. Expires 5 minutes after the refusal.

The retry is sent either with the same request body (`system`, `messages`,
`tools`, and other render-shaping fields), or with the same body plus one
appended `assistant` message whose content is the partial text (with any
trailing whitespace stripped from the final text block) and paired
server-tool blocks from this refusal вЂ” which also authorizes that
appended turn as an assistant-prefill continuation on models that otherwise
disallow prefill. A token minted mid-server-tool-loop whose partial content
was continuable may only be redeemed the second way вЂ” if a same-body retry
is rejected with a 400 saying the token must be redeemed by continuing the
partial response, retry the second way instead. Either way: same workspace,
same platform; a mismatch is a 400. Resending a token for an already-warm
prefix is permitted but yields no additional credit.

`null` when the refused model isn't eligible for a fallback credit.

оЂЄ

fallback\_has\_prefill\_claim: Optional[bool]о‚љ

Whether the accompanying `fallback_credit_token` may be redeemed with the
appended-assistant retry form. Only set when `fallback_credit_token` is
present.

`true`: retry by resending the same request body plus one appended
`assistant` message whose content is this response's `content` with any
trailing whitespace stripped from the final text block and unpaired
`tool_use` blocks omitted (the same appended-turn shape described on
`fallback_credit_token`), with the token attached. `false`: retry by
resending the original request body unchanged, with the token attached вЂ”
the appended-assistant form is not available for this refusal (no
continuable partial content, or the request uses `output_format` or a
`tool_choice` that forces tool use). One exception: when the request used
`output_format` or a forced `tool_choice` and the refusal arrived after
server tools (including MCP connector tools) had already executed, the
token may not be redeemable by either retry form; if the exact-body retry
is then rejected with a 400 saying the token must be redeemed by
continuing the partial response, discard the token and retry without it.

Advisory: if an appended-assistant retry is rejected with a 400 despite
`true`, fall back to resending the original request body with the token.

recommended\_model: Optional[str]о‚љ

The server's suggested retry target for this refusal. Populated when a fallback attempt could not be made (the fallback model's rate limit was exhausted, or it was overloaded); names the fallback model the caller can retry directly. Null otherwise.

type: Literal["refusal"]о‚љ

оЂЄ

stop\_reason: Optional[BetaStopReason]о‚љ

The reason that we stopped.

This may be one the following values:

- `"end_turn"`: the model reached a natural stopping point
- `"max_tokens"`: we exceeded the requested `max_tokens` or the model's maximum
- `"stop_sequence"`: one of your provided custom `stop_sequences` was generated
- `"tool_use"`: the model invoked one or more tools
- `"pause_turn"`: we paused a long-running turn. You may provide the response back as-is in a subsequent request to let the model continue.
- `"refusal"`: when streaming classifiers intervene to handle potential policy violations

In non-streaming mode this value is always non-null. In streaming mode, it is null in the `message_start` event and non-null otherwise.

One of the following:

"end\_turn"о‚љ

"max\_tokens"о‚љ

"stop\_sequence"о‚љ

"tool\_use"о‚љ

"pause\_turn"о‚љ

"compaction"о‚љ

"refusal"о‚љ

"model\_context\_window\_exceeded"о‚љ

оЂЄ

stop\_sequence: Optional[str]о‚љ

Which custom stop sequence was generated, if any.

This value will be a non-null string if one of your custom stop sequences was generated.

оЂЄ

type: Literal["message"]о‚љ

Object type.

For Messages, this is always `"message"`.

оЂЄ

usage: [BetaUsage](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Billing and rate-limit usage.

Anthropic's API bills and rate-limits by token counts, as tokens represent the underlying cost to our systems.

Under the hood, the API transforms requests into a format suitable for the model. The model's output then goes through a parsing stage before becoming an API response. As a result, the token counts in `usage` will not match one-to-one with the exact visible content of an API request or response.

For example, `output_tokens` will be non-zero, even for an empty string response from Claude.

Total input tokens in a request is the summation of `input_tokens`, `cache_creation_input_tokens`, and `cache_read_input_tokens`.

оЂЄ

cache\_creation: Optional[BetaCacheCreation]о‚љ

Breakdown of cached tokens by TTL

ephemeral\_1h\_input\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the 1 hour cache entry.

ephemeral\_5m\_input\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the 5 minute cache entry.

cache\_creation\_input\_tokens: Optional[int]о‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the cache entry.

cache\_read\_input\_tokens: Optional[int]о‚љ

The number of input tokens read from the cache.

inference\_geo: Optional[str]о‚љ

The geographic region where inference was performed for this request.

input\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of input tokens which were used.

оЂЄ

iterations: Optional[BetaIterationsUsage]о‚љ

Per-iteration token usage breakdown.

Each entry represents one sampling iteration, with its own input/output token counts and cache statistics. This allows you to:

- Determine which iterations exceeded long context thresholds (>=200k tokens)
- Calculate the true context window size from the last iteration
- Understand token accumulation across server-side tool use loops

One of the following:

оЂЄ

class BetaMessageIterationUsage: вЂ¦о‚љ

Token usage for a sampling iteration.

оЂЄ

cache\_creation: Optional[BetaCacheCreation]о‚љ

Breakdown of cached tokens by TTL

ephemeral\_1h\_input\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the 1 hour cache entry.

ephemeral\_5m\_input\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the 5 minute cache entry.

cache\_creation\_input\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the cache entry.

cache\_read\_input\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of input tokens read from the cache.

input\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of input tokens which were used.

оЂЄ

class BetaFallbackMessageIterationUsage: вЂ¦о‚љ

Token usage for the fallback-model attempt of a server-side fallback request.

Produced in place of a `message` entry for whichever hop served the
response. A declined hop produces the existing `message` entry. Whether
a fallback model served the response is signalled by the presence of this
entry in `usage.iterations`.

оЂЄ

cache\_creation: Optional[BetaCacheCreation]о‚љ

Breakdown of cached tokens by TTL

ephemeral\_1h\_input\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the 1 hour cache entry.

ephemeral\_5m\_input\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the 5 minute cache entry.

cache\_creation\_input\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the cache entry.

cache\_read\_input\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of input tokens read from the cache.

input\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of input tokens which were used.

strо‚љ

output\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of output tokens which were used.

type: Literal["fallback\_message"]о‚љ

Usage for the fallback-model attempt that served the response

output\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of output tokens which were used.

оЂЄ

output\_tokens\_details: Optional[BetaOutputTokensDetails]о‚љ

Breakdown of output tokens by category.

`output_tokens` remains the inclusive, authoritative total used for billing.
This object provides a read-only decomposition for observability вЂ” for example,
how many of the billed output tokens were spent on internal reasoning that may
have been summarized before being returned to you.

оЂЄ

thinking\_tokens: intо‚љ

Number of output tokens the model generated as internal reasoning, including
the thinking-block delimiter tokens.

Reflects the raw reasoning the model produced, not the (possibly shorter)
summarized thinking text returned in the response body. Computed by
re-tokenizing the raw reasoning text, so it may differ from the model's exact
generation count by a small number of tokens. Always в‰¤ `output_tokens`;
`output_tokens - thinking_tokens` approximates the non-reasoning output.

minimum0

The number of web search tool requests.

оЂЄ

canceled: intо‚љ

Number of requests in the Message Batch that have been canceled.

This is zero until processing of the entire Message Batch has ended.

оЂЄ

errored: intо‚љ

Number of requests in the Message Batch that encountered an error.

This is zero until processing of the entire Message Batch has ended.

оЂЄ

expired: intо‚љ

Number of requests in the Message Batch that have expired.

This is zero until processing of the entire Message Batch has ended.

processing: intо‚љ

Number of requests in the Message Batch that are processing.

оЂЄ

succeeded: intо‚љ

Number of requests in the Message Batch that have completed successfully.

This is zero until processing of the entire Message Batch has ended.

оЂЄ

Contains a Message output if processing was successful, an error response if processing failed, or the reason why processing was not attempted, such as cancellation or expiration.

оЂЄ

content: List[[BetaContentBlock](api/beta.md)]о‚љ

Content generated by the model.

This is an array of content blocks, each of which has a `type` that determines its shape.

Example:

```shiki
[{"type": "text", "text": "Hi, I'm Claude."}]
```

оЃ–

If the request input `messages` ended with an `assistant` turn, then the response `content` will continue directly from that last turn. You can use this to constrain the model's output.

For example, if the input `messages` were:

```shiki
[
  {"role": "user", "content": "What's the Greek name for Sun? (A) Sol (B) Helios (C) Sun"},
  {"role": "assistant", "content": "The best answer is ("}
]
```

оЃ–

Then the response `content` might be:

```shiki
[{"type": "text", "text": "B)"}]
```

оЃ–

One of the following:

оЂЄ

class BetaTextBlock: вЂ¦о‚љ

оЂЄ

citations: Optional[List[[BetaTextCitation](api/beta.md)]]о‚љ

Citations supporting the text block.

The type of citation returned will depend on the type of document being cited. Citing a PDF results in `page_location`, plain text results in `char_location`, and content document results in `content_block_location`.

One of the following:

оЂЄ

class BetaCitationCharLocation: вЂ¦о‚љ

document\_index: intо‚љ

оЂЄ

class BetaCitationContentBlockLocation: вЂ¦о‚љ

оЂЄ

cited\_text: strо‚љ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

document\_index: intо‚љ

document\_title: Optional[str]о‚љ

оЂЄ

end\_block\_index: intо‚љ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

file\_id: Optional[str]о‚љ

start\_block\_index: intо‚љ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

type: Literal["content\_block\_location"]о‚љ

оЂЄ

encrypted\_index: strо‚љ

title: Optional[str]о‚љ

type: Literal["web\_search\_result\_location"]о‚љ

url: strо‚љ

class BetaCitationSearchResultLocation: вЂ¦о‚љ

оЂЄ

cited\_text: strо‚љ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

оЂЄ

end\_block\_index: intо‚љ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

оЂЄ

search\_result\_index: intо‚љ

0-based index of the cited search result among all `search_result` content blocks in the request, in the order they appear across messages and tool results.

Counted separately from `document_index`; server-side web search results are not included in this count.

minimum0

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

citations: Optional[List[[BetaTextCitation](api/beta.md)]]о‚љ

Citations supporting the text block.

The type of citation returned will depend on the type of document being cited. Citing a PDF results in `page_location`, plain text results in `char_location`, and content document results in `content_block_location`.

One of the following:

оЂЄ

document\_index: intо‚љ

class BetaCitationContentBlockLocation: вЂ¦о‚љ

оЂЄ

cited\_text: strо‚љ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

document\_index: intо‚љ

document\_title: Optional[str]о‚љ

оЂЄ

end\_block\_index: intо‚љ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

file\_id: Optional[str]о‚љ

start\_block\_index: intо‚љ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

type: Literal["content\_block\_location"]о‚љ

оЂЄ

class BetaCitationSearchResultLocation: вЂ¦о‚љ

оЂЄ

cited\_text: strо‚љ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

оЂЄ

end\_block\_index: intо‚љ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

оЂЄ

search\_result\_index: intо‚љ

0-based index of the cited search result among all `search_result` content blocks in the request, in the order they appear across messages and tool results.

Counted separately from `document_index`; server-side web search results are not included in this count.

minimum0

class BetaCompactionBlock: вЂ¦о‚љ

A compaction block returned when autocompact is triggered.

When content is None, it indicates the compaction failed to produce a valid
summary (e.g., malformed output from the model). Clients may round-trip
compaction blocks with null content; the server treats them as no-ops.

content: Optional[str]о‚љ

Summary of compacted content, or null if compaction failed

encrypted\_content: Optional[str]о‚љ

Opaque metadata from prior compaction, to be round-tripped verbatim

type: Literal["compaction"]о‚љ

оЂЄ

class BetaFallbackBlock: вЂ¦о‚љ

Marks the point in `content` where one model's output gives way to the next.

One block appears per hop where a preceding model actually ran this turn and
declined. A turn routed directly by the sticky decision has no such boundary
and carries no block вЂ” the signal for whether a fallback model served the
response is the presence of a `fallback_message` entry in
`usage.iterations`, not this block.

The block is treated like a server-tool content block for streaming: it
arrives via the standard `content_block_start` / `content_block_stop`
pair and carries no deltas.

оЂЄ

from\_: [BetaFallbackInfo](api/beta.md)о‚љ

The model whose output ends at this point вЂ” the model that declined at this hop. When the declining hop is the requested model, its `model` echoes the top-level `model` string the caller sent (alias or canonical); when the declining hop is a fallback model, its `model` is that model's canonical id.

оЂЄ

Information about context management strategies applied during the request.

оЂЄ

applied\_edits: List[AppliedEdit]о‚љ

List of context management edits that were applied.

One of the following:

оЂЄ

class BetaClearToolUses20250919EditResponse: вЂ¦о‚љ

The type of context management edit applied.

оЂЄ

The type of context management edit applied.

diagnostics: Optional[BetaDiagnostics]о‚љ

Response envelope for request-level diagnostics. Present (possibly
null) whenever the caller supplied `diagnostics` on the request.

оЂЄ

cache\_miss\_reason: Optional[CacheMissReason]о‚љ

Explains why the prompt cache could not fully reuse the prefix from the request identified by `diagnostics.previous_message_id`. `null` means diagnosis is still pending вЂ” the response was serialized before the background comparison completed.

strо‚љ

оЂЄ

role: Literal["assistant"]о‚љ

Conversational role of the generated message.

This will always be `"assistant"`.

оЂЄ

stop\_details: Optional[BetaRefusalStopDetails]о‚љ

Structured information about a refusal.

оЂЄ

category: Optional[Literal["cyber", "bio", "reasoning\_extraction"]]о‚љ

The policy category that triggered the refusal.

`null` when the refusal doesn't map to a named category.

One of the following:

"cyber"о‚љ

"bio"о‚љ

"reasoning\_extraction"о‚љ

оЂЄ

explanation: Optional[str]о‚љ

Human-readable explanation of the refusal.

This text is not guaranteed to be stable. `null` when no explanation is available for the category.

оЂЄ

fallback\_credit\_token: Optional[str]о‚љ

Opaque code that refunds the cache-miss cost when retrying this refused
request on the fallback model. Pass it as `fallback_credit_token` on the
retry request. Expires 5 minutes after the refusal.

The retry is sent either with the same request body (`system`, `messages`,
`tools`, and other render-shaping fields), or with the same body plus one
appended `assistant` message whose content is the partial text (with any
trailing whitespace stripped from the final text block) and paired
server-tool blocks from this refusal вЂ” which also authorizes that
appended turn as an assistant-prefill continuation on models that otherwise
disallow prefill. A token minted mid-server-tool-loop whose partial content
was continuable may only be redeemed the second way вЂ” if a same-body retry
is rejected with a 400 saying the token must be redeemed by continuing the
partial response, retry the second way instead. Either way: same workspace,
same platform; a mismatch is a 400. Resending a token for an already-warm
prefix is permitted but yields no additional credit.

`null` when the refused model isn't eligible for a fallback credit.

оЂЄ

fallback\_has\_prefill\_claim: Optional[bool]о‚љ

Whether the accompanying `fallback_credit_token` may be redeemed with the
appended-assistant retry form. Only set when `fallback_credit_token` is
present.

`true`: retry by resending the same request body plus one appended
`assistant` message whose content is this response's `content` with any
trailing whitespace stripped from the final text block and unpaired
`tool_use` blocks omitted (the same appended-turn shape described on
`fallback_credit_token`), with the token attached. `false`: retry by
resending the original request body unchanged, with the token attached вЂ”
the appended-assistant form is not available for this refusal (no
continuable partial content, or the request uses `output_format` or a
`tool_choice` that forces tool use). One exception: when the request used
`output_format` or a forced `tool_choice` and the refusal arrived after
server tools (including MCP connector tools) had already executed, the
token may not be redeemable by either retry form; if the exact-body retry
is then rejected with a 400 saying the token must be redeemed by
continuing the partial response, discard the token and retry without it.

Advisory: if an appended-assistant retry is rejected with a 400 despite
`true`, fall back to resending the original request body with the token.

recommended\_model: Optional[str]о‚љ

The server's suggested retry target for this refusal. Populated when a fallback attempt could not be made (the fallback model's rate limit was exhausted, or it was overloaded); names the fallback model the caller can retry directly. Null otherwise.

type: Literal["refusal"]о‚љ

оЂЄ

stop\_reason: Optional[BetaStopReason]о‚љ

The reason that we stopped.

This may be one the following values:

- `"end_turn"`: the model reached a natural stopping point
- `"max_tokens"`: we exceeded the requested `max_tokens` or the model's maximum
- `"stop_sequence"`: one of your provided custom `stop_sequences` was generated
- `"tool_use"`: the model invoked one or more tools
- `"pause_turn"`: we paused a long-running turn. You may provide the response back as-is in a subsequent request to let the model continue.
- `"refusal"`: when streaming classifiers intervene to handle potential policy violations

In non-streaming mode this value is always non-null. In streaming mode, it is null in the `message_start` event and non-null otherwise.

One of the following:

"end\_turn"о‚љ

"max\_tokens"о‚љ

"stop\_sequence"о‚љ

"tool\_use"о‚љ

"pause\_turn"о‚љ

"compaction"о‚љ

"refusal"о‚љ

"model\_context\_window\_exceeded"о‚љ

оЂЄ

stop\_sequence: Optional[str]о‚љ

Which custom stop sequence was generated, if any.

This value will be a non-null string if one of your custom stop sequences was generated.

оЂЄ

type: Literal["message"]о‚љ

Object type.

For Messages, this is always `"message"`.

оЂЄ

usage: [BetaUsage](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Billing and rate-limit usage.

Anthropic's API bills and rate-limits by token counts, as tokens represent the underlying cost to our systems.

Under the hood, the API transforms requests into a format suitable for the model. The model's output then goes through a parsing stage before becoming an API response. As a result, the token counts in `usage` will not match one-to-one with the exact visible content of an API request or response.

For example, `output_tokens` will be non-zero, even for an empty string response from Claude.

Total input tokens in a request is the summation of `input_tokens`, `cache_creation_input_tokens`, and `cache_read_input_tokens`.

оЂЄ

cache\_creation: Optional[BetaCacheCreation]о‚љ

Breakdown of cached tokens by TTL

ephemeral\_1h\_input\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the 1 hour cache entry.

ephemeral\_5m\_input\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the 5 minute cache entry.

cache\_creation\_input\_tokens: Optional[int]о‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the cache entry.

cache\_read\_input\_tokens: Optional[int]о‚љ

The number of input tokens read from the cache.

inference\_geo: Optional[str]о‚љ

The geographic region where inference was performed for this request.

input\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of input tokens which were used.

оЂЄ

iterations: Optional[BetaIterationsUsage]о‚љ

Per-iteration token usage breakdown.

Each entry represents one sampling iteration, with its own input/output token counts and cache statistics. This allows you to:

- Determine which iterations exceeded long context thresholds (>=200k tokens)
- Calculate the true context window size from the last iteration
- Understand token accumulation across server-side tool use loops

One of the following:

оЂЄ

class BetaMessageIterationUsage: вЂ¦о‚љ

Token usage for a sampling iteration.

оЂЄ

cache\_creation: Optional[BetaCacheCreation]о‚љ

Breakdown of cached tokens by TTL

ephemeral\_1h\_input\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the 1 hour cache entry.

ephemeral\_5m\_input\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the 5 minute cache entry.

cache\_creation\_input\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the cache entry.

cache\_read\_input\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of input tokens read from the cache.

input\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of input tokens which were used.

оЂЄ

class BetaFallbackMessageIterationUsage: вЂ¦о‚љ

Token usage for the fallback-model attempt of a server-side fallback request.

Produced in place of a `message` entry for whichever hop served the
response. A declined hop produces the existing `message` entry. Whether
a fallback model served the response is signalled by the presence of this
entry in `usage.iterations`.

оЂЄ

cache\_creation: Optional[BetaCacheCreation]о‚љ

Breakdown of cached tokens by TTL

ephemeral\_1h\_input\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the 1 hour cache entry.

ephemeral\_5m\_input\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the 5 minute cache entry.

cache\_creation\_input\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the cache entry.

cache\_read\_input\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of input tokens read from the cache.

input\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of input tokens which were used.

strо‚љ

output\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of output tokens which were used.

type: Literal["fallback\_message"]о‚љ

Usage for the fallback-model attempt that served the response

output\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of output tokens which were used.

оЂЄ

output\_tokens\_details: Optional[BetaOutputTokensDetails]о‚љ

Breakdown of output tokens by category.

`output_tokens` remains the inclusive, authoritative total used for billing.
This object provides a read-only decomposition for observability вЂ” for example,
how many of the billed output tokens were spent on internal reasoning that may
have been summarized before being returned to you.

оЂЄ

thinking\_tokens: intо‚љ

Number of output tokens the model generated as internal reasoning, including
the thinking-block delimiter tokens.

Reflects the raw reasoning the model produced, not the (possibly shorter)
summarized thinking text returned in the response body. Computed by
re-tokenizing the raw reasoning text, so it may differ from the model's exact
generation count by a small number of tokens. Always в‰¤ `output_tokens`;
`output_tokens - thinking_tokens` approximates the non-reasoning output.

minimum0

web\_fetch\_requests: intо‚љ

web\_search\_requests: intо‚љ

The number of web search tool requests.

content: List[[BetaContentBlock](api/beta.md)]о‚љ

Content generated by the model.

This is an array of content blocks, each of which has a `type` that determines its shape.

Example:

```shiki
[{"type": "text", "text": "Hi, I'm Claude."}]
```

оЃ–

If the request input `messages` ended with an `assistant` turn, then the response `content` will continue directly from that last turn. You can use this to constrain the model's output.

For example, if the input `messages` were:

```shiki
[
  {"role": "user", "content": "What's the Greek name for Sun? (A) Sol (B) Helios (C) Sun"},
  {"role": "assistant", "content": "The best answer is ("}
]
```

оЃ–

Then the response `content` might be:

```shiki
[{"type": "text", "text": "B)"}]
```

оЃ–

One of the following:

оЂЄ

class BetaTextBlock: вЂ¦о‚љ

оЂЄ

citations: Optional[List[[BetaTextCitation](api/beta.md)]]о‚љ

Citations supporting the text block.

The type of citation returned will depend on the type of document being cited. Citing a PDF results in `page_location`, plain text results in `char_location`, and content document results in `content_block_location`.

One of the following:

оЂЄ

class BetaCitationCharLocation: вЂ¦о‚љ

cited\_text: strо‚љ

document\_index: intо‚љ

document\_title: Optional[str]о‚љ

end\_char\_index: intо‚љ

file\_id: Optional[str]о‚љ

start\_char\_index: intо‚љ

type: Literal["char\_location"]о‚љ

оЂЄ

class BetaCitationPageLocation: вЂ¦о‚љ

document\_index: intо‚љ

document\_title: Optional[str]о‚љ

end\_page\_number: intо‚љ

file\_id: Optional[str]о‚љ

start\_page\_number: intо‚љ

type: Literal["page\_location"]о‚љ

оЂЄ

class BetaCitationContentBlockLocation: вЂ¦о‚љ

оЂЄ

cited\_text: strо‚љ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

document\_index: intо‚љ

document\_title: Optional[str]о‚љ

оЂЄ

end\_block\_index: intо‚љ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

file\_id: Optional[str]о‚љ

start\_block\_index: intо‚љ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

type: Literal["content\_block\_location"]о‚љ

оЂЄ

encrypted\_index: strо‚љ

title: Optional[str]о‚љ

type: Literal["web\_search\_result\_location"]о‚љ

url: strо‚љ

class BetaCitationSearchResultLocation: вЂ¦о‚љ

оЂЄ

cited\_text: strо‚љ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

оЂЄ

end\_block\_index: intо‚љ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

оЂЄ

search\_result\_index: intо‚љ

0-based index of the cited search result among all `search_result` content blocks in the request, in the order they appear across messages and tool results.

Counted separately from `document_index`; server-side web search results are not included in this count.

minimum0

citations: Optional[List[[BetaTextCitation](api/beta.md)]]о‚љ

Citations supporting the text block.

The type of citation returned will depend on the type of document being cited. Citing a PDF results in `page_location`, plain text results in `char_location`, and content document results in `content_block_location`.

One of the following:

оЂЄ

document\_index: intо‚љ

start\_char\_index: intо‚љ

type: Literal["page\_location"]о‚љ

оЂЄ

class BetaCitationContentBlockLocation: вЂ¦о‚љ

оЂЄ

cited\_text: strо‚љ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

document\_index: intо‚љ

document\_title: Optional[str]о‚љ

оЂЄ

end\_block\_index: intо‚љ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

file\_id: Optional[str]о‚љ

start\_block\_index: intо‚љ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

type: Literal["content\_block\_location"]о‚љ

оЂЄ

cited\_text: strо‚љ

encrypted\_index: strо‚љ

title: Optional[str]о‚љ

type: Literal["web\_search\_result\_location"]о‚љ

url: strо‚љ

оЂЄ

class BetaCitationSearchResultLocation: вЂ¦о‚љ

оЂЄ

cited\_text: strо‚љ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

оЂЄ

end\_block\_index: intо‚љ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

оЂЄ

search\_result\_index: intо‚љ

0-based index of the cited search result among all `search_result` content blocks in the request, in the order they appear across messages and tool results.

Counted separately from `document_index`; server-side web search results are not included in this count.

minimum0

source: strо‚љ

start\_block\_index: intо‚љ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

title: Optional[str]о‚љ

type: Literal["search\_result\_location"]о‚љ

class BetaCompactionBlock: вЂ¦о‚љ

A compaction block returned when autocompact is triggered.

When content is None, it indicates the compaction failed to produce a valid
summary (e.g., malformed output from the model). Clients may round-trip
compaction blocks with null content; the server treats them as no-ops.

content: Optional[str]о‚љ

Summary of compacted content, or null if compaction failed

encrypted\_content: Optional[str]о‚љ

Opaque metadata from prior compaction, to be round-tripped verbatim

type: Literal["compaction"]о‚љ

оЂЄ

class BetaFallbackBlock: вЂ¦о‚љ

Marks the point in `content` where one model's output gives way to the next.

One block appears per hop where a preceding model actually ran this turn and
declined. A turn routed directly by the sticky decision has no such boundary
and carries no block вЂ” the signal for whether a fallback model served the
response is the presence of a `fallback_message` entry in
`usage.iterations`, not this block.

The block is treated like a server-tool content block for streaming: it
arrives via the standard `content_block_start` / `content_block_stop`
pair and carries no deltas.

оЂЄ

from\_: [BetaFallbackInfo](api/beta.md)о‚љ

The model whose output ends at this point вЂ” the model that declined at this hop. When the declining hop is the requested model, its `model` echoes the top-level `model` string the caller sent (alias or canonical); when the declining hop is a fallback model, its `model` is that model's canonical id.

оЂЄ

Explains why the prompt cache could not fully reuse the prefix from the request identified by `diagnostics.previous_message_id`. `null` means diagnosis is still pending вЂ” the response was serialized before the background comparison completed.

strо‚љ

оЂЄ

role: Literal["assistant"]о‚љ

Conversational role of the generated message.

This will always be `"assistant"`.

оЂЄ

stop\_details: Optional[BetaRefusalStopDetails]о‚љ

Structured information about a refusal.

оЂЄ

category: Optional[Literal["cyber", "bio", "reasoning\_extraction"]]о‚љ

The policy category that triggered the refusal.

`null` when the refusal doesn't map to a named category.

One of the following:

"cyber"о‚љ

"bio"о‚љ

"reasoning\_extraction"о‚љ

оЂЄ

explanation: Optional[str]о‚љ

Human-readable explanation of the refusal.

This text is not guaranteed to be stable. `null` when no explanation is available for the category.

оЂЄ

fallback\_credit\_token: Optional[str]о‚љ

Opaque code that refunds the cache-miss cost when retrying this refused
request on the fallback model. Pass it as `fallback_credit_token` on the
retry request. Expires 5 minutes after the refusal.

The retry is sent either with the same request body (`system`, `messages`,
`tools`, and other render-shaping fields), or with the same body plus one
appended `assistant` message whose content is the partial text (with any
trailing whitespace stripped from the final text block) and paired
server-tool blocks from this refusal вЂ” which also authorizes that
appended turn as an assistant-prefill continuation on models that otherwise
disallow prefill. A token minted mid-server-tool-loop whose partial content
was continuable may only be redeemed the second way вЂ” if a same-body retry
is rejected with a 400 saying the token must be redeemed by continuing the
partial response, retry the second way instead. Either way: same workspace,
same platform; a mismatch is a 400. Resending a token for an already-warm
prefix is permitted but yields no additional credit.

`null` when the refused model isn't eligible for a fallback credit.

оЂЄ

fallback\_has\_prefill\_claim: Optional[bool]о‚љ

Whether the accompanying `fallback_credit_token` may be redeemed with the
appended-assistant retry form. Only set when `fallback_credit_token` is
present.

`true`: retry by resending the same request body plus one appended
`assistant` message whose content is this response's `content` with any
trailing whitespace stripped from the final text block and unpaired
`tool_use` blocks omitted (the same appended-turn shape described on
`fallback_credit_token`), with the token attached. `false`: retry by
resending the original request body unchanged, with the token attached вЂ”
the appended-assistant form is not available for this refusal (no
continuable partial content, or the request uses `output_format` or a
`tool_choice` that forces tool use). One exception: when the request used
`output_format` or a forced `tool_choice` and the refusal arrived after
server tools (including MCP connector tools) had already executed, the
token may not be redeemable by either retry form; if the exact-body retry
is then rejected with a 400 saying the token must be redeemed by
continuing the partial response, discard the token and retry without it.

Advisory: if an appended-assistant retry is rejected with a 400 despite
`true`, fall back to resending the original request body with the token.

recommended\_model: Optional[str]о‚љ

The server's suggested retry target for this refusal. Populated when a fallback attempt could not be made (the fallback model's rate limit was exhausted, or it was overloaded); names the fallback model the caller can retry directly. Null otherwise.

type: Literal["refusal"]о‚љ

оЂЄ

stop\_reason: Optional[BetaStopReason]о‚љ

The reason that we stopped.

This may be one the following values:

- `"end_turn"`: the model reached a natural stopping point
- `"max_tokens"`: we exceeded the requested `max_tokens` or the model's maximum
- `"stop_sequence"`: one of your provided custom `stop_sequences` was generated
- `"tool_use"`: the model invoked one or more tools
- `"pause_turn"`: we paused a long-running turn. You may provide the response back as-is in a subsequent request to let the model continue.
- `"refusal"`: when streaming classifiers intervene to handle potential policy violations

In non-streaming mode this value is always non-null. In streaming mode, it is null in the `message_start` event and non-null otherwise.

One of the following:

"end\_turn"о‚љ

"max\_tokens"о‚љ

"stop\_sequence"о‚љ

"tool\_use"о‚љ

"pause\_turn"о‚љ

"compaction"о‚љ

"refusal"о‚љ

"model\_context\_window\_exceeded"о‚љ

оЂЄ

stop\_sequence: Optional[str]о‚љ

Which custom stop sequence was generated, if any.

This value will be a non-null string if one of your custom stop sequences was generated.

оЂЄ

type: Literal["message"]о‚љ

Object type.

For Messages, this is always `"message"`.

оЂЄ

usage: [BetaUsage](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Billing and rate-limit usage.

Anthropic's API bills and rate-limits by token counts, as tokens represent the underlying cost to our systems.

Under the hood, the API transforms requests into a format suitable for the model. The model's output then goes through a parsing stage before becoming an API response. As a result, the token counts in `usage` will not match one-to-one with the exact visible content of an API request or response.

For example, `output_tokens` will be non-zero, even for an empty string response from Claude.

Total input tokens in a request is the summation of `input_tokens`, `cache_creation_input_tokens`, and `cache_read_input_tokens`.

оЂЄ

cache\_creation: Optional[BetaCacheCreation]о‚љ

Breakdown of cached tokens by TTL

ephemeral\_1h\_input\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the 1 hour cache entry.

ephemeral\_5m\_input\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the 5 minute cache entry.

cache\_creation\_input\_tokens: Optional[int]о‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the cache entry.

cache\_read\_input\_tokens: Optional[int]о‚љ

The number of input tokens read from the cache.

inference\_geo: Optional[str]о‚љ

The geographic region where inference was performed for this request.

input\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of input tokens which were used.

оЂЄ

iterations: Optional[BetaIterationsUsage]о‚љ

Per-iteration token usage breakdown.

Each entry represents one sampling iteration, with its own input/output token counts and cache statistics. This allows you to:

- Determine which iterations exceeded long context thresholds (>=200k tokens)
- Calculate the true context window size from the last iteration
- Understand token accumulation across server-side tool use loops

One of the following:

оЂЄ

class BetaMessageIterationUsage: вЂ¦о‚љ

Token usage for a sampling iteration.

оЂЄ

cache\_creation: Optional[BetaCacheCreation]о‚љ

Breakdown of cached tokens by TTL

ephemeral\_1h\_input\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the 1 hour cache entry.

ephemeral\_5m\_input\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the 5 minute cache entry.

cache\_creation\_input\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the cache entry.

cache\_read\_input\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of input tokens read from the cache.

input\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of input tokens which were used.

оЂЄ

class BetaFallbackMessageIterationUsage: вЂ¦о‚љ

Token usage for the fallback-model attempt of a server-side fallback request.

Produced in place of a `message` entry for whichever hop served the
response. A declined hop produces the existing `message` entry. Whether
a fallback model served the response is signalled by the presence of this
entry in `usage.iterations`.

оЂЄ

The number of input tokens used to create the 5 minute cache entry.

cache\_creation\_input\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the cache entry.

cache\_read\_input\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of input tokens read from the cache.

input\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of input tokens which were used.

output\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of output tokens which were used.

type: Literal["fallback\_message"]о‚љ

Usage for the fallback-model attempt that served the response

output\_tokens: intо‚љ

The number of output tokens which were used.

оЂЄ

output\_tokens\_details: Optional[BetaOutputTokensDetails]о‚љ

Breakdown of output tokens by category.

`output_tokens` remains the inclusive, authoritative total used for billing.
This object provides a read-only decomposition for observability вЂ” for example,
how many of the billed output tokens were spent on internal reasoning that may
have been summarized before being returned to you.

оЂЄ

thinking\_tokens: intо‚љ

Number of output tokens the model generated as internal reasoning, including
the thinking-block delimiter tokens.

Reflects the raw reasoning the model produced, not the (possibly shorter)
summarized thinking text returned in the response body. Computed by
re-tokenizing the raw reasoning text, so it may differ from the model's exact
generation count by a small number of tokens. Always в‰¤ `output_tokens`;
`output_tokens - thinking_tokens` approximates the non-reasoning output.

minimum0

web\_search\_requests: intо‚љ

The number of web search tool requests.

#### BetaAgents

##### [Create Agent](api/beta/agents/create.md)

beta.agents.create(AgentCreateParams\*\*kwargs)  -> [BetaManagedAgentsAgent](api/beta.md)

POST/v1/agents

##### [List Agents](api/beta/agents/list.md)

beta.agents.list(AgentListParams\*\*kwargs)  -> SyncPageCursor[[BetaManagedAgentsAgent](api/beta.md)]

GET/v1/agents

##### [Get Agent](api/beta/agents/retrieve.md)

beta.agents.retrieve(stragent\_id, AgentRetrieveParams\*\*kwargs)  -> [BetaManagedAgentsAgent](api/beta.md)

GET/v1/agents/{agent\_id}

##### [Update Agent](api/beta/agents/update.md)

beta.agents.update(stragent\_id, AgentUpdateParams\*\*kwargs)  -> [BetaManagedAgentsAgent](api/beta.md)

POST/v1/agents/{agent\_id}

##### [Archive Agent](api/beta/agents/archive.md)

beta.agents.archive(stragent\_id, AgentArchiveParams\*\*kwargs)  -> [BetaManagedAgentsAgent](api/beta.md)

POST/v1/agents/{agent\_id}/archive

class BetaManagedAgentsAgentToolset20260401BashInput: вЂ¦о‚љ

Input payload for the `bash` tool of the
`agent_toolset_20260401` toolset. All fields are optional;
a normal invocation supplies `command`, while `restart=true`
(with no `command`) reboots the runner-side bash session.

command: Optional[str]о‚љ

Shell command to execute. Omit only when `restart` is true.

restart: Optional[bool]о‚љ

When true, restart the persistent bash session instead of
running a command. Subsequent calls without `restart` will
run against the fresh session.

timeout\_ms: Optional[int]о‚љ

Per-call timeout in milliseconds. Defaults to the
runner-wide tool timeout when omitted or zero.

оЂЄ

class BetaManagedAgentsAgentToolset20260401EditInput: вЂ¦о‚љ

Input payload for the `edit` tool. Performs a string
replacement in the named file; by default `old_string` must
occur exactly once.

file\_path: strо‚љ

Path of the file to edit.

new\_string: strо‚љ

Replacement text.

old\_string: strо‚љ

Substring to find and replace.

replace\_all: Optional[bool]о‚љ

When true, replace every occurrence of `old_string`
instead of requiring a unique match.

оЂЄ

class BetaManagedAgentsAgentToolset20260401GlobInput: вЂ¦о‚љ

Input payload for the `glob` tool. Returns paths matching a
doublestar glob pattern, newest first.

pattern: strо‚љ

Doublestar glob pattern (e.g. `**/*.go`). Absolute patterns
are only permitted when the runner is configured to allow
them.

path: Optional[str]о‚љ

Optional directory root to search under. Defaults to the
runner's working directory.

оЂЄ

class BetaManagedAgentsAgentToolset20260401GrepInput: вЂ¦о‚љ

Input payload for the `grep` tool. Searches file contents for
a regular expression, returning matching lines.

pattern: strо‚љ

Regular expression to search for.

path: Optional[str]о‚љ

Optional directory root to search under. Defaults to the
runner's working directory.

оЂЄ

class BetaManagedAgentsCustomToolParams: вЂ¦о‚љ

A custom tool that is executed by the API client rather than the agent. When the agent calls this tool, an `agent.custom_tool_use` event is emitted and the session goes idle, waiting for the client to provide the result via a `user.custom_tool_result` event.

description: strо‚љ

Description of what the tool does, shown to the agent to help it decide when to use the tool. 1-1024 characters.

оЂЄ

input\_schema: [BetaManagedAgentsCustomToolInputSchema](api/beta.md)о‚љ

JSON Schema for custom tool input parameters.

type: Literal["object"]о‚љ

properties: Optional[Dict[str, object]]о‚љ

required: Optional[List[str]]о‚љ

name: strо‚љ

Unique name for the tool. 1-128 characters; letters, digits, underscores, and hyphens.

type: Literal["custom"]о‚љ

Literal["claude-fable-5", "claude-opus-4-8", "claude-opus-4-7", 8 more]о‚љ

The model that will power your agent.

See [models](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/models-overview) for additional details and options.

- `claude-fable-5` - Next generation of intelligence for the hardest knowledge work and coding problems
- `claude-opus-4-8` - Frontier intelligence for long-running agents and coding
- `claude-opus-4-7` - Frontier intelligence for long-running agents and coding
- `claude-opus-4-6` - Most intelligent model for building agents and coding
- `claude-sonnet-4-6` - Best combination of speed and intelligence
- `claude-haiku-4-5` - Fastest model with near-frontier intelligence
- `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001` - Fastest model with near-frontier intelligence
- `claude-opus-4-5` - Premium model combining maximum intelligence with practical performance
- `claude-opus-4-5-20251101` - Premium model combining maximum intelligence with practical performance
- `claude-sonnet-4-5` - High-performance model for agents and coding
- `claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929` - High-performance model for agents and coding

class BetaManagedAgentsMultiagentCoordinator: вЂ¦о‚љ

Resolved coordinator topology with a concrete agent roster.

оЂЄ

agents: List[[BetaManagedAgentsAgentReference](api/beta.md)]о‚љ

Agents the coordinator may spawn as session threads, each resolved to a specific version.

id: strо‚љ

type: Literal["agent"]о‚љ

version: intо‚љ

type: Literal["coordinator"]о‚љ

оЂЄ

class BetaManagedAgentsMultiagentCoordinatorParams: вЂ¦о‚љ

A coordinator topology: the session's primary thread orchestrates work by spawning session threads, each running an agent drawn from the `agents` roster.

оЂЄ

agents: List[[BetaManagedAgentsMultiagentRosterEntryParams](api/beta.md)]о‚љ

Agents the coordinator may spawn as session threads. 1вЂ“20 entries. Each entry is an agent ID string, a versioned `{"type":"agent","id","version"}` reference, or `{"type":"self"}` to allow recursive self-invocation. Entries must reference distinct agents (after resolving `self` and string forms); at most one `self`. Referenced agents must exist, must not be archived, and must not themselves have `multiagent` set (depth limit 1).

One of the following:

strо‚љ

оЂЄ

class BetaManagedAgentsAgentParams: вЂ¦о‚љ

Specification for an Agent. Provide a specific `version` or use the short-form `agent="agent_id"` for the most recent version

id: strо‚љ

The `agent` ID.

type: Literal["agent"]о‚љ

version: Optional[int]о‚љ

The specific `agent` version to use. Omit to use the latest version. Must be at least 1 if specified.

оЂЄ

class BetaManagedAgentsMultiagentSelfParams: вЂ¦о‚љ

Sentinel roster entry meaning "the agent that owns this configuration". Resolved server-side to a concrete agent reference.

type: Literal["self"]о‚љ

type: Literal["coordinator"]о‚љ

оЂЄ

class BetaManagedAgentsMultiagentSelfParams: вЂ¦о‚љ

Sentinel roster entry meaning "the agent that owns this configuration". Resolved server-side to a concrete agent reference.

type: Literal["self"]о‚љ

оЂЄ

class BetaManagedAgentsSessionThreadAgent: вЂ¦о‚љ

Resolved `agent` definition for a single `session_thread`. Snapshot of the agent at thread creation time. The multiagent roster is not repeated here; read it from `Session.agent`.

id: strо‚љ

description: Optional[str]о‚љ

оЂЄ

name: strо‚љ

type: Literal["url"]о‚љ

url: strо‚љ

оЂЄ

class BetaSelfHostedWork: вЂ¦о‚љ

Work resource representing a unit of work in a self-hosted environment.

Work items are queued when sessions are created or when long-dormant sessions
receive new messages. The environment worker polls for work to execute in a
self-hosted sandbox.

id: strо‚љ

Work identifier (e.g., 'work\_...')

acknowledged\_at: Optional[str]о‚љ

RFC 3339 timestamp when the work item was acknowledged and assigned to a self-hosted sandbox

created\_at: strо‚љ

RFC 3339 timestamp when work was created

оЂЄ

data: [BetaSessionWorkData](api/beta.md)о‚љ

The actual work to be performed

id: strо‚љ

Session identifier (e.g., 'session\_...')

type: Literal["session"]о‚љ

Type of work data

environment\_id: strо‚љ

Environment identifier this work belongs to (e.g., `env_...`)

latest\_heartbeat\_at: Optional[str]о‚љ

RFC 3339 timestamp of the most recent heartbeat

metadata: Dict[str, str]о‚љ

User-provided metadata key-value pairs associated with this work item

started\_at: Optional[str]о‚љ

RFC 3339 timestamp when work execution started

оЂЄ

state: Literal["queued", "starting", "active", 2 more]о‚љ

Current state of the work item

One of the following:

"queued"о‚љ

"starting"о‚љ

"active"о‚љ

"stopping"о‚љ

"stopped"о‚љ

stop\_requested\_at: Optional[str]о‚љ

RFC 3339 timestamp when stop was requested

stopped\_at: Optional[str]о‚љ

RFC 3339 timestamp when work execution stopped

type: Literal["work"]о‚љ

The type of object (always 'work')

оЂЄ

class BetaSelfHostedWorkHeartbeatResponse: вЂ¦о‚љ

Response after recording a heartbeat for a work item.

last\_heartbeat: strо‚љ

RFC 3339 timestamp of the actual heartbeat from DB

lease\_extended: boolо‚љ

Whether the heartbeat succeeded in extending the lease

оЂЄ

state: Literal["queued", "starting", "active", 2 more]о‚љ

Current state of the work item (active/stopping/stopped)

One of the following:

"queued"о‚љ

"starting"о‚љ

"active"о‚љ

"stopping"о‚љ

"stopped"о‚љ

ttl\_seconds: intо‚љ

Effective TTL applied to the lease

type: Literal["work\_heartbeat"]о‚љ

The type of response

class BetaSelfHostedWorkQueueStats: вЂ¦о‚љ

Statistics about the work queue for an environment.

Uses Redis Stream consumer group metrics for O(1) queries.

depth: intо‚љ

Number of work items waiting to be picked up (lag from consumer group)

oldest\_queued\_at: Optional[str]о‚љ

RFC 3339 timestamp of oldest item in the work stream (includes both queued and pending items), null if stream empty

pending: intо‚љ

Number of work items being processed (polled but not acknowledged)

type: Literal["work\_queue\_stats"]о‚љ

The type of object

workers\_polling: Optional[int]о‚љ

Number of workers that have polled for work in the last 30 seconds. Requires worker\_id to be sent with poll requests.

оЂЄ

class BetaSelfHostedWorkStopRequest: вЂ¦о‚љ

Request to stop a work item.

force: Optional[bool]о‚љ

If true, immediately stop work without graceful shutdown

оЂЄ

class BetaSelfHostedWorkUpdateRequest: вЂ¦о‚љ

Request to update work item metadata.

metadata: Dict[str, Optional[str]]о‚љ

Metadata patch. Set a key to a string to upsert it, or to null to delete it. Omit the field to preserve existing metadata.

Agents the coordinator may spawn as session threads, each resolved to a specific version.

id: strо‚љ

type: Literal["agent"]о‚љ

version: intо‚љ

type: Literal["coordinator"]о‚љ

оЂЄ

class BetaManagedAgentsMultiagentParams: вЂ¦о‚љ

A coordinator topology: the session's primary thread orchestrates work by spawning session threads, each running an agent drawn from the `agents` roster.

оЂЄ

agents: Sequence[[BetaManagedAgentsMultiagentRosterEntryParams](api/beta.md)]о‚љ

Agents the coordinator may spawn as session threads. 1вЂ“20 entries. Each entry is an agent ID string, a versioned `{"type":"agent","id","version"}` reference, or `{"type":"self"}` to allow recursive self-invocation. Entries must reference distinct agents (after resolving `self` and string forms); at most one `self`. Referenced agents must exist, must not be archived, and must not themselves have `multiagent` set (depth limit 1).

One of the following:

strо‚љ

оЂЄ

class BetaManagedAgentsAgentParams: вЂ¦о‚љ

Specification for an Agent. Provide a specific `version` or use the short-form `agent="agent_id"` for the most recent version

id: strо‚љ

The `agent` ID.

type: Literal["agent"]о‚љ

version: Optional[int]о‚љ

The specific `agent` version to use. Omit to use the latest version. Must be at least 1 if specified.

оЂЄ

class BetaManagedAgentsMultiagentSelfParams: вЂ¦о‚љ

Sentinel roster entry meaning "the agent that owns this configuration". Resolved server-side to a concrete agent reference.

type: Literal["self"]о‚љ

type: Literal["coordinator"]о‚љ

оЂЄ

[BetaManagedAgentsMultiagentRosterEntryParams](api/beta.md)о‚љ

An entry in a multiagent roster: an agent ID string, a versioned agent reference, or `self`.

One of the following:

strо‚љ

оЂЄ

class BetaManagedAgentsAgentParams: вЂ¦о‚љ

Specification for an Agent. Provide a specific `version` or use the short-form `agent="agent_id"` for the most recent version

id: strо‚љ

The `agent` ID.

type: Literal["agent"]о‚љ

version: Optional[int]о‚љ

The specific `agent` version to use. Omit to use the latest version. Must be at least 1 if specified.

оЂЄ

class BetaManagedAgentsMultiagentSelfParams: вЂ¦о‚љ

Sentinel roster entry meaning "the agent that owns this configuration". Resolved server-side to a concrete agent reference.

type: Literal["self"]о‚љ

оЂЄ

class BetaManagedAgentsOutcomeEvaluationResource: вЂ¦о‚љ

Evaluation state for a single outcome defined via a define\_outcome event.

completed\_at: Optional[datetime]о‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

description: strо‚љ

What the agent should produce.

explanation: Optional[str]о‚љ

Grader's verdict text from the most recent evaluation. For satisfied, explains why criteria are met; for needs\_revision (intermediate), what's missing; for failed, why unrecoverable.

iteration: intо‚љ

0-indexed revision cycle the outcome is currently on.

outcome\_id: strо‚љ

Server-generated outc\_ ID for this outcome.

result: strо‚љ

Current evaluation state. `pending` before the agent begins work; `running` while producing or revising; `evaluating` while the grader scores; `satisfied`/`max_iterations_reached`/`failed`/`interrupted` are terminal.

type: Literal["outcome\_evaluation"]о‚љ

оЂЄ

Literal["claude-fable-5", "claude-opus-4-8", "claude-opus-4-7", 8 more]о‚љ

The model that will power your agent.

See [models](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/models-overview) for additional details and options.

- `claude-fable-5` - Next generation of intelligence for the hardest knowledge work and coding problems
- `claude-opus-4-8` - Frontier intelligence for long-running agents and coding
- `claude-opus-4-7` - Frontier intelligence for long-running agents and coding
- `claude-opus-4-6` - Most intelligent model for building agents and coding
- `claude-sonnet-4-6` - Best combination of speed and intelligence
- `claude-haiku-4-5` - Fastest model with near-frontier intelligence
- `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001` - Fastest model with near-frontier intelligence
- `claude-opus-4-5` - Premium model combining maximum intelligence with practical performance
- `claude-opus-4-5-20251101` - Premium model combining maximum intelligence with practical performance
- `claude-sonnet-4-5` - High-performance model for agents and coding
- `claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929` - High-performance model for agents and coding

"fast"о‚љ

class BetaManagedAgentsMemoryStoreResource: вЂ¦о‚љ

A memory store attached to an agent session.

memory\_store\_id: strо‚љ

The memory store ID (memstore\_...). Must belong to the caller's organization and workspace.

type: Literal["memory\_store"]о‚љ

оЂЄ

access: Optional[Literal["read\_write", "read\_only"]]о‚љ

Access mode for an attached memory store.

One of the following:

"read\_write"о‚љ

"read\_only"о‚љ

description: Optional[str]о‚љ

Description of the memory store, snapshotted at attach time. Rendered into the agent's system prompt. Empty string when the store has no description.

instructions: Optional[str]о‚љ

Per-attachment guidance for the agent on how to use this store. Rendered into the memory section of the system prompt. Max 4096 chars.

mount\_path: Optional[str]о‚љ

Filesystem path where the store is mounted in the session container, e.g. /mnt/memory/user-preferences. Derived from the store's name. Output-only.

name: Optional[str]о‚љ

Display name of the memory store, snapshotted at attach time. Later edits to the store's name do not propagate to this resource.

оЂЄ

stats: [BetaManagedAgentsSessionStats](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Timing statistics for a session.

active\_seconds: Optional[float]о‚љ

Cumulative time in seconds the session spent in running status. Excludes idle time.

duration\_seconds: Optional[float]о‚љ

Elapsed time since session creation in seconds. For terminated sessions, frozen at the final update.

оЂЄ

status: Literal["rescheduling", "running", "idle", "terminated"]о‚љ

SessionStatus enum

One of the following:

"rescheduling"о‚љ

"running"о‚љ

"idle"о‚љ

"terminated"о‚љ

title: Optional[str]о‚љ

type: Literal["session"]о‚љ

updated\_at: datetimeо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

оЂЄ

usage: [BetaManagedAgentsSessionUsage](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Cumulative token usage for a session across all turns.

оЂЄ

cache\_creation: Optional[BetaManagedAgentsCacheCreationUsage]о‚љ

Prompt-cache creation token usage broken down by cache lifetime.

ephemeral\_1h\_input\_tokens: Optional[int]о‚љ

Tokens used to create 1-hour ephemeral cache entries.

ephemeral\_5m\_input\_tokens: Optional[int]о‚љ

Tokens used to create 5-minute ephemeral cache entries.

cache\_read\_input\_tokens: Optional[int]о‚љ

Total tokens read from prompt cache.

input\_tokens: Optional[int]о‚љ

Total input tokens consumed across all turns.

output\_tokens: Optional[int]о‚љ

Total output tokens generated across all turns.

vault\_ids: List[str]о‚љ

Vault IDs attached to the session at creation. Empty when no vaults were supplied.

deployment\_id: Optional[str]о‚љ

Deployment ID when the session was created from a deployment reference. Null otherwise.

- `claude-fable-5` - Next generation of intelligence for the hardest knowledge work and coding problems
- `claude-opus-4-8` - Frontier intelligence for long-running agents and coding
- `claude-opus-4-7` - Frontier intelligence for long-running agents and coding
- `claude-opus-4-6` - Most intelligent model for building agents and coding
- `claude-sonnet-4-6` - Best combination of speed and intelligence
- `claude-haiku-4-5` - Fastest model with near-frontier intelligence
- `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001` - Fastest model with near-frontier intelligence
- `claude-opus-4-5` - Premium model combining maximum intelligence with practical performance
- `claude-opus-4-5-20251101` - Premium model combining maximum intelligence with practical performance
- `claude-sonnet-4-5` - High-performance model for agents and coding
- `claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929` - High-performance model for agents and coding

class BetaManagedAgentsSessionAgentUpdate: вЂ¦о‚љ

Mid-session agent configuration update. Only `tools` and `mcp_servers` are updatable. Full replacement: the provided array becomes the new value. To preserve existing entries, GET the session, modify the array, and POST it back.

оЂЄ

mcp\_servers: Optional[List[[BetaManagedAgentsURLMCPServerParams](api/beta.md)]]о‚љ

Replacement MCP server list. Full replacement: the provided array becomes the new value. Send an empty array to clear; omit to preserve.

name: strо‚љ

Unique name for this server, referenced by mcp\_toolset configurations. 1-255 characters.

type: Literal["url"]о‚љ

url: strо‚љ

Endpoint URL for the MCP server.

оЂЄ

tools: Optional[List[Tool]]о‚љ

Replacement tool list. Full replacement: the provided array becomes the new value. Send an empty array to clear; omit to preserve.

One of the following:

class BetaManagedAgentsCustomToolParams: вЂ¦о‚љ

A custom tool that is executed by the API client rather than the agent. When the agent calls this tool, an `agent.custom_tool_use` event is emitted and the session goes idle, waiting for the client to provide the result via a `user.custom_tool_result` event.

description: strо‚љ

Description of what the tool does, shown to the agent to help it decide when to use the tool. 1-1024 characters.

оЂЄ

input\_schema: [BetaManagedAgentsCustomToolInputSchema](api/beta.md)о‚љ

JSON Schema for custom tool input parameters.

type: Literal["object"]о‚љ

properties: Optional[Dict[str, object]]о‚љ

required: Optional[List[str]]о‚љ

name: strо‚љ

Unique name for the tool. 1-128 characters; letters, digits, underscores, and hyphens.

type: Literal["custom"]о‚љ

Timing statistics for a session.

active\_seconds: Optional[float]о‚љ

Cumulative time in seconds the session spent in running status. Excludes idle time.

duration\_seconds: Optional[float]о‚љ

Elapsed time since session creation in seconds. For terminated sessions, frozen at the final update.

class BetaManagedAgentsSessionUpdatedEvent: вЂ¦о‚љ

Emitted when an UpdateSession request changed at least one field. Carries only the fields that changed; absent fields were not part of the update. The new configuration applies from the next turn.

id: strо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

processed\_at: datetimeо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

type: Literal["session.updated"]о‚љ

Literal["claude-fable-5", "claude-opus-4-8", "claude-opus-4-7", 8 more]о‚љ

The model that will power your agent.

See [models](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/models-overview) for additional details and options.

- `claude-fable-5` - Next generation of intelligence for the hardest knowledge work and coding problems
- `claude-opus-4-8` - Frontier intelligence for long-running agents and coding
- `claude-opus-4-7` - Frontier intelligence for long-running agents and coding
- `claude-opus-4-6` - Most intelligent model for building agents and coding
- `claude-sonnet-4-6` - Best combination of speed and intelligence
- `claude-haiku-4-5` - Fastest model with near-frontier intelligence
- `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001` - Fastest model with near-frontier intelligence
- `claude-opus-4-5` - Premium model combining maximum intelligence with practical performance
- `claude-opus-4-5-20251101` - Premium model combining maximum intelligence with practical performance
- `claude-sonnet-4-5` - High-performance model for agents and coding
- `claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929` - High-performance model for agents and coding

Cumulative token usage for a session across all turns.

Prompt-cache creation token usage broken down by cache lifetime.

class BetaManagedAgentsUserToolResultEvent: вЂ¦о‚љ

Event sent by the client providing the result of an agent-toolset tool execution. Only valid on `self_hosted` environments, where sandbox-routed tools are executed by the client rather than the server.

#### BetaSessionsEvents

##### [List Events](api/beta/sessions/events/list.md)

beta.sessions.events.list(strsession\_id, EventListParams\*\*kwargs)  -> SyncPageCursor[[BetaManagedAgentsSessionEvent](api/beta.md)]

GET/v1/sessions/{session\_id}/events

##### [Send Events](api/beta/sessions/events/send.md)

class BetaManagedAgentsAgentCustomToolUseEvent: вЂ¦о‚љ

Event emitted when the agent calls a custom tool. The session goes idle until the client sends a `user.custom_tool_result` event with the result.

id: strо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

input: Dict[str, object]о‚љ

Input parameters for the tool call.

name: strо‚љ

Name of the custom tool being called.

processed\_at: datetimeо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

type: Literal["agent.custom\_tool\_use"]о‚љ

session\_thread\_id: Optional[str]о‚љ

When set, this event was cross-posted from a subagent's thread to surface its custom tool use on the primary thread's stream. Empty on the thread's own events. Echo this on a `user.custom_tool_result` event to route the result back.

class BetaManagedAgentsBillingError: вЂ¦о‚љ

The caller's organization or workspace cannot make model requests вЂ” out of credits or spend limit reached. Retrying with the same credentials will not succeed; the caller must resolve the billing state.

message: strо‚љ

Human-readable error description.

оЂЄ

retry\_status: RetryStatusо‚љ

What the client should do next in response to this error.

One of the following:

оЂЄ

class BetaManagedAgentsRetryStatusRetrying: вЂ¦о‚љ

The server is retrying automatically. Client should wait; the same error type may fire again as retrying, then once as exhausted when the retry budget runs out.

type: Literal["retrying"]о‚љ

оЂЄ

class BetaManagedAgentsRetryStatusExhausted: вЂ¦о‚љ

This turn is dead; queued inputs are flushed and the session returns to idle. Client may send a new prompt.

type: Literal["exhausted"]о‚љ

оЂЄ

class BetaManagedAgentsRetryStatusTerminal: вЂ¦о‚љ

The session encountered a terminal error and will transition to `terminated` state.

type: Literal["terminal"]о‚љ

type: Literal["billing\_error"]о‚љ

оЂЄ

class BetaManagedAgentsCredentialHostUnreachableError: вЂ¦о‚љ

An `environment_variable` credential's `auth.networking.allowed_hosts` includes a host the environment's network policy does not permit.

credential\_id: strо‚љ

ID of the affected credential.

message: strо‚љ

Human-readable error description.

оЂЄ

retry\_status: RetryStatusо‚љ

What the client should do next in response to this error.

One of the following:

оЂЄ

class BetaManagedAgentsRetryStatusRetrying: вЂ¦о‚љ

The server is retrying automatically. Client should wait; the same error type may fire again as retrying, then once as exhausted when the retry budget runs out.

type: Literal["retrying"]о‚љ

оЂЄ

class BetaManagedAgentsRetryStatusExhausted: вЂ¦о‚љ

This turn is dead; queued inputs are flushed and the session returns to idle. Client may send a new prompt.

type: Literal["exhausted"]о‚љ

оЂЄ

class BetaManagedAgentsRetryStatusTerminal: вЂ¦о‚љ

The session encountered a terminal error and will transition to `terminated` state.

type: Literal["terminal"]о‚љ

type: Literal["credential\_host\_unreachable\_error"]о‚љ

vault\_id: strо‚љ

ID of the vault containing the affected credential.

"deny"о‚љ

tool\_use\_id: strо‚љ

The id of the `agent.tool_use` or `agent.mcp_tool_use` event this result corresponds to, which can be found in the last `session.status_idle` [event's](api/beta/sessions/events/list.md) `stop_reason.event_ids` field.
